questions.question: Do cats have more toes on their front or back paws?
Name two of the four people mentioned in the Kenny Loggins song Footloose.
Which word isn't an acronym: laser, scuba or velcro?
Andy Warhol said, "I used to have the same lunch every day for 20 years." What was it?
In internet book clubs, what does DNF stand for?
Which band won Song of the Year at the 2026 APRA Awards for a second year running?
Ljubljana is the capital of which central European country?
Which of these is a Scottish folk dance: Bon Odori, Hambone, Strip the Willow?
The rhomboid muscles connect the vertebrae with which bones?
In which 2000 film does George Clooney's character use Dapper Dan hair pomade?
Which country's sovereign sea territories include the Dardanelles and Bosphorus Straits?
Match the dog to the book: Buck, Skulker, Pongo; Wuthering Heights, The Hundred and One Dalmatians, The Call of the Wild.
questions.answer: Buck (The Call of the Wild), Skulker (Wuthering Heights), Pongo (The Hundred and One Dalmatians)

questions.question: Is the human circulatory system approximately 100, 10,000 or 100,000 kilometres long?
Which two NRL teams have birds in their names?
Who has replaced Aaron Chen as the assistant on ABC TV game show Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont-Spelling Bee?
Used by molluscs to coat the interior of their shells, nacre is more commonly known as… what?
In which year did Hotmail launch, Prince Charles and Diana, Princess of Wales, divorce and the Spice Girls release the song Wannabe?
What does the c represent in Einstein's equation E=mc²?
questions.answer: The speed of light in a vacuum

questions.question: In which country is the Nagarjuna Sagar Dam, the world's tallest masonry dam?
Which typically has the most strings: a banjo, guitar or mandolin?
Which annual event began in 1903 to promote L'Auto, a sports newspaper?
Which is the lightest element in the periodic table?
What is unique about Vincent Van Gogh's 1888 painting, The Red Vineyards near Arles?
questions.answer: It was the only one of his works sold during his lifetime

questions.question: Which English city is named for its Roman-built spa facilities?
Which US war film is loosely based on Joseph Conrad's novella, Heart of Darkness?
